text:
American College for Physicians (ACP)

Location: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States

ACP is a diverse, global community of internal medicine specialists and subspecialists united by a commitment to excellence. ACP's goal is to provide clinicians with recommendations based on the best available evidence; to inform clinicians of when there is no evidence; and finally, to help clinicians deliver the best health care possible.

Webpage

Recommendations

Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ)

Location: Rockville, Maryland, United States

The purpose of the AHRQ is to enhance the quality, appropriateness, and effectiveness of health services, and access to such services through the establishment of a broad base of scientific research and through the promotion of improvements in clinical and health system practices, including the prevention of diseases and other health conditions. 

Webpage

Recommendations

Alliance for the Quality of Mental Health Care (Akwa GGZ)

Location: Utrecht, Netherlands

Akwa GGZ is an alliance of professional, industry, and patient associations that facilitates learning mental health professionals in improving the quality of care for patients and their families. This alliance develops and maintains care standards, facilitates learning networks, and creates mirror information for organizations based on existing data. The combination of care standards, data, and practical experience strengthens the development of quality care. 

Webpage

Recommendations

Associação Médica Brasileira (AMB, Brazilian Medical Association) 

Location: Sao Paulo, Brazil

The AMB plays an active role in technical committees, councils, and national forums, collaborating with government agencies, universities, and research institutions in the formulation of public policies, clinical guidelines, and parameters for quality of care, ensuring the presence of scientific medicine in strategic health decisions in Brazil. It periodically publishes a series of original publications, including guidelines, developed to support medical practice and give visibility to its main initiatives among its members, federated entities, and specialty societies. Since 2000, the Guidelines Project has collaborated with Specialty Societies to develop recommendations based on the best available scientific evidence.

Webpage

Recommendations

American Psychiatric Association (APA)

Location: Washington DC, United States

As the leading psychiatric organization in the world, APA has more than 40,000 members involved in psychiatric practice, research, and academia representing the diversity of the patients for whom they care. APA Clinical Practice Guidelines provide evidence-based recommendations for the assessment and treatment of psychiatric disorders and are intended to assist in clinical decision making by presenting systematically developed patient care strategies in a standardized format.

Webpage

Recommendations

American Psychological Association (APA Psy)

Location: Washington DC, United States

APA Psy is the leading scientific and professional organization representing psychology in the United States, with more than 190,000 researchers, educators, clinicians, consultants, and students as its members. Their mission is to promote the advancement, communication, and application of psychological science and knowledge to benefit society and improve lives.  

Webpage

Recommendations

Australian Psychological Society (APS)

Location: Melbourne, Australia

APS is working to improve the lives of Australians through psychology, working in diverse settings and sectors around the country. It helps the community to understand and access psychology via education, advocacy, and evidence-based practice. APS Position Statements outline key recommendations based on psychological evidence about contemporary social issues, such as climate change, racism and gambling.

Webpage

Recommendations

Arbeitsgemeinschaft der Wissenschaftlichen Medizinischen Fachgesellschaften (AWMF, Association of Scientific Medical Societies)

Location: Marburg, Germany

The AWMF comprises more than 180 specialist societies from all areas of scientific medicine, representing over 300,000 experts with specialized research and work focuses. It represents the interests of medical science and communicates them to policymakers and the public, advocating for evidence-based medicine. It addresses interdisciplinary issues in scientific medicine, promotes collaboration among its members in pursuing shared tasks and goals, and facilitates the transfer of scientific findings into clinical practice. The Institute for Medical Knowledge Management (IMWi) coordinates the publication of recommendations for action in the healthcare sector, i.e., guidelines. To shape healthcare in the interest of patients, the system of medical guidelines is continuously being further developed digitally.

Webpage

Recommendations

Bangladesh Association of Psychiatrists (BAP)

Location: Dhaka, Bangladesh

The BAP represents the largest association of mental health professionals in Bangladesh and works on promoting ethical and evidence-based psychiatric practice, health advocacy, policy formulation and medical research in the field of mental health. It has developed several guidelines for the management of mental disorders.

Webpage

Recommendations

British Columbia Government (BCG)

Location: Victoria, BC, Canada

The British Columbia offers clinical practice guidelines and protocols that provide recommendations to B.C. practitioners on delivering high quality, appropriate care to patients with specific clinical conditions or diseases. 

Webpage

Recommendations

Canadian Network form Mood and Anxiety Treatments (CANMAT)

Location: Vancouver, Canada

The CANMAT is a network of academic and clinical experts dedicated to improving clinical care for people with mood and anxiety disorders. They provide up-to-date scientific information, treatment guidelines and educational activities for health professionals, and clear and useful information about symptoms and treatments for patients and families.

Webpage

Recommendations

Chilean Ministry of Health (Chilean MoH)

Location: Santiago, Chile

DIPRECE contributes to improving the quality and level of health of the population by leading the formulation, monitoring and evaluation of public policies, plans and programs in disease prevention and control, exercising a leading and regulatory role that considers the approaches of rights, equity, gender, participation and that addresses the social determinants and health needs of the population based on the best available evidence. One of their contributions consists of developing Clinical Guidelines.

Webpage

Recommendations

Cochrane Collaboration (Cochrane)

Location: London, United Kingdom

Cochrane is a global, independent, non-profit network of health researchers and professionals, patients and carers who work together to produce and promote trusted, high-quality health information that improves health and healthcare worldwide.

Webpage

Recommendations

Colombian Ministry of Health (Colombian MoH)

Location: Bogotá, Colombia

The Colombian MoH is the national government authority responsible for developing and implementing health and social protection policies in Colombia. It oversees the country's healthcare system, regulates health services, promotes public health, and coordinates the development of clinical practice guidelines and health technologies.

Webpage

Recommendations

Česká psychoterapeutická společnost. České lékařské společnosti JPE (CPTS, Czech Psychotherapeutic Society of the Czech Medical Society)

Location: Prague, Czech Republic

The ČPtS is committed to the development and expansion of psychotherapy. Its aim is to apply it in medical care and in the preventive mental health care of citizens. For that, they have developed standards for several disorders.

Webpage

Recommendations

Dansk Psykiatrisk Selskab (DPS, Danish Psychiatric Association) 

Location: Copenhagen, Denmark

The DPS is a medical society whose main task is to promote Danish psychiatry and research in this field. One way of doing that is by creating Guidelines.

Webpage

Recommendations

The Finnish Medical Society Duodecim (Duodecim)

Location: Helsinki, Finland

The Duodecim is Finland’s largest scientific association. Its aim is to develop the professional skills of doctors and support their clinical practice through continuing education, publications and research grants. Their Current Treatment Recommendations are independent, national treatment recommendations based on research evidence that address important issues related to the health of Finns and the treatment and prevention of diseases.

Webpage

Recommendations

European Psychiatric Association (EPA)

Location: Strasbourg, France

The EPA is the main association representing psychiatry in Europe. The EPA's activities represent the interests of psychiatrists in academia, research and practice throughout all stages of career development. The EPA Guidance Papers are expert-authored papers that share best practices and evidence-based insights aimed at enhancing mental health care quality across Europe. Commissioned by the EPA Board, they help foster cross-country learning, especially in areas where formal guidelines are lacking.

Webpage

Recommendations

Eswatinian Ministry of Health (Eswatinian MoH)

Location: Mbabane, Eswatini

The Eswatinian MoH's mission is to improve the health of the people of Swaziland by providing leadership in the production, delivery and utilization of health services which will consistently increase longevity and quality of life. The Standard Treatment Guideline and Essential Medicines List of Common Medical Conditions in the Kingdom of Swaziland consists in systematically developed statements designed to assist practitioners in making decisions about appropriate treatment for specific clinical conditions.

Webpage

Recommendations

Federatie Médisch Specialisten (FMS, Federation of Specialist Physicians)

Location: Utrecht, Netherlands

The FMS is driven by the ambition of every medical specialist to provide the best possible care to every patient. They unite medical specialists and scientific societies, collaborate on the practice and development of the profession, and speak with one powerful voice in politics and society. Their Guidelines Database contains medical guidelines with evidence-based recommendations for daily practice for secondary and tertiary care.

Webpage

Recommendations

GuiaSalud (GS, Health Guidelines)

Location: Zaragoza, Spain

GuiaSalud is an organization of the Spanish National Health System in which the 17 Autonomous Communities and the Ministry of Health participate. It serves to provide the development of a system to help in decision-making based on scientific evidence, facilitating collaborative work and knowledge management in the field of healthcare and clinical research.

Webpage

Recommendations

High Authority for Health (HAS)

Location: Paris, France

The HAS works to ensure that care and support at every stage of life are safe, effective and tailored to each individual. It evaluates health technologies from a clinical and health economic perspective; makes recommendations for health, social and medico-social professionals, in order to optimize and harmonize their practices and organizations; is responsible for measuring and improving the quality of care and patient safety in healthcare facilities and primary care settings, as well as the quality of support provided in social and medico-social establishments and services.

Webpage

Recommendations

Instituto de Evaluación de Tecnologías en Salud e Investigación (IETSI, Institute for Health Technology Assessment and Research)

Location: Lima, Peru

The IETSI is the decentralized body of the Social Health Insurance, responsible for the evaluation of health technologies for their incorporation, suppression, use or change in the institution; and in turn it is in charge of proposing the norms and strategies for scientific and technological innovation; in addition to promoting and regulating scientific research in the field of health; developing and approving the clinical practice guidelines in the institution and implementing the pharmacovigilance and technovigilance system.

Webpage

Recommendations

Instituto Mexicano del Seguro Social (IMSS, Mexican Institute for Social Security)

Location: Mexico City, Mexico

The IMSS is the institution with the greatest presence in healthcare and social protection for Mexicans since its founding. To this end, it combines medical research and practice with the administration of retirement funds for its insured members, providing peace of mind and stability to workers and their families in the face of any of the risks specified in the Social Security Law.

Webpage

Recommendations

National Institute of Excellence in Health and Social Services (INESSS)

Location: Montreal, Canada

The INESSS mission is to promote clinical excellence and the efficient use of resources in the health and social services sector.

Webpage

Recommendations

Instituto Nacional de Salud (INS, National Health Institute)

Location: Lima, Peru

The INS develops scientific research to dynamically understand the health situation in Peru and to promote the development of technological interventions that effectively and promptly address the public health needs and demands of the population, as part of the work of the Ministry of Health.

Webpage

Recommendations

Indian Psychiatry Society (IPS)

Location: Gurgaon, India

The IPS is the principal professional association representing psychiatrists in India. It promotes excellence in psychiatric practice, education, research, advocacy, and mental health policy. It has been publishing systematically developed patient care strategies for effective clinical decision making and delivery of care since 2005. These guidelines are intended for managing clinical situations that psychiatrists generally encounter in practice.

Webpage

Recommendations

Belgian Health Care Knowledge Centre (KCE)

Location: Brussels, Belgium

KCE is an independent research centre that provides scientific advice on topics related to healthcare. The topics are generally submitted by the authorities, universities, professional associations, etc. KCE’s mission is to advise policymakers on decisions relating to healthcare and health insurance on the basis of scientific and objective research. It is expected to propose the best possible solutions for an accessible, high-quality healthcare system with due regard for growing demand and budgetary constraints.

Webpage

Recommendations

Ministry of Health of North Macedonia (Macedonian MoH)

Location: Skopje, North Macedonia

The Macedonian MoH s the government body responsible for developing, implementing, and overseeing health policy and the public healthcare system in North Macedonia. It operates under the Government of North Macedonia and is responsible for ensuring access to healthcare, regulating health services, and protecting public health. The Ministry develops and publishes national clinical guidelines and protocols for a wide range of medical conditions, often in collaboration with national expert committees and professional medical societies. These guidelines aim to standardize healthcare delivery across the country and promote evidence-based clinical practice.

Webpage

Recommendations

Malaysian Ministry of Health (MaHTAS)

Location: Putrajaya, Malaysia

The Malaysian Health Technology Assessment Section (MaHTAS), under the Medical Development Division, is the Ministry of Health’s custodian of evidence- and value-based medicine. MaHTAS produces health technology assessment reports through systematic, multidisciplinary evaluations of health technologies and interventions. These reports provide vital insights to guide Ministry of Health policies and inform the public. We also develop and implement clinical practice guidelines based on the best available evidence, helping healthcare providers deliver higher quality patient care.

Webpage

Recommendations

The Matrix

Location: Edinburgh, United Kingdom

The MatriX is designed to support evidence-based practice of the delivery of psychological therapies and interventions. The ambition is that everyone has timely access to high quality, person-centred, evidence-based psychological therapies and interventions that effectively improve health, mental health and wellbeing when needed by the people of Scotland.  The Matrix supports this by providing guidance on the evidence for effective and accessible delivery. Its goal is to ensure the right therapy or intervention, is available at the right time, delivered by a well-trained and supported workforce, and provided as efficiently as possible.

Webpage

Recommendations

MediX

Location: Zurich, Switzerland

The association mediX Schweitz is a network of ten regional physician networks. It connects innovative physician networks; represents the interests of the affiliated networks and the association; develops medical guidelines and recommendations in line with the desired care structure and quality assurance and enables all licensees to communicate their shared care culture using the mediX brand.

Webpage

Recommendations

Michigan Medicine: University of Michigan (MM)

Location: Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States

MM is the University of Michigan’s academic medical center, where they work to transform lives through a combination of ground-breaking research, expert clinical care and leading-edge medical and scientific education. Their policies include Guidelines for the treatment of multiple health concerns.

Webpage

Recommendations

Nederlands Huisarten Genootschap (NHG, Dutch College of General Practitioners)

Location: Utrecht, Netherlands

The NHG is the professional and scientific organization for general practitioners in the Netherlands. It aims at improving the quality of primary healthcare. For that, the NHG creates evidence-based clinical guidelines and standards that are followed by Dutch GPs in their daily practice. It also facilitates medical education, training, and research in the field of general practice. This way, the NHG helps in the application of science in practice.

Webpage

Recommendations

National Institute for Health Care Excellence (NICE)

Location: Manchester, United Kingdom

NICE helps practitioners and commissioners get the best care to people, fast, while ensuring value for the taxpayer. They do this by producing useful and usable guidance for health and care practitioners, which includes rigorous, independent assessment of complex evidence for new health technologies. They develop recommendations that focus on what matters most and drive innovation into the hands of health and care practitioners.

Webpage

Recommendations

Psychiatrická společnost České lékařské společnosti J. E. Purkyně (PSCLS, Psychiatric Society of the Czchek Medical Society)

Location: Prague, Czech Republic

The Czech Psychiatric Society is a civil association that closely cooperates with the Psychiatric Society of the Czech Republic JEP, whose aim is to develop the field of psychiatry and whose main objectives and activities include the publication and co-publication of professional printed materials, publications, and journals

Webpage

Recommendations

Qatari Ministry of Public Health (Qatari MoH)

Location: Doha, Qatar

The Ministry of Public Health of Qatar is the government authority responsible for public health policy, regulation, and stewardship of Qatar's healthcare system. It develops national health strategies, regulates healthcare providers and professionals, oversees public health initiatives, and coordinates healthcare delivery across the country. One of the Ministry's major initiatives is the National Clinical Guidelines (NCG) Development Program, which aims to standardize evidence-based clinical practice across Qatar's healthcare system.

Webpage

Recommendations

Royal Australian and New Zealand College of Psychiatrists (RANZCP)

Location: Melbourne, Australia

The RANZCP is responsible for training, educating and representing psychiatrists in Australia and Aotearoa New Zealand. The RANZCP produces guidance to help psychiatrists and other mental health professionals in their work.

Webpage

Recommendations

Ministry of Heath of the Russian Federation (Russian MoH)

Location: Moscow, Russia

The Russian MoH responsible for public health policy, regulation, and the overall health system in Russia. Its mission encompasses promoting health, preventing diseases, and enhancing the quality of medical services across the nation. The Ministry oversees the development and approval of national clinical guidelines for a broad range of medical conditions. These guidelines are primarily developed by national professional medical societies and expert groups, then reviewed and approved through a formal process coordinated by the Ministry.

Webpage

Recommendations

Andalusian Regional Health Service (SAS)

Location: Seville, Spain

The Andalusian Regional Health Service's mission is to provide healthcare to the citizens of Andalusia, offering quality public health services, ensuring accessibility, equity and user satisfaction, seeking efficiency and optimal use of resources.

Webpage

Recommendations

Society for Clinical Psychology (SCP)

Location: Atlanta, United States

A division of the American Psychological Association that promotes the integration of psychological science throughout all areas of practice, including clinical service provision, research, education, and advocacy. It offers a list of psychological treatments supported by published efficacy data, based on their criteria (Chambless & Hollon, 1998; Tolin, 2015). It is designed to guide treatment decisions by integrating research evidence with clinician expertise and patient preferences and is intended for a broad audience including the public, clinicians, educators, researchers, and students.

Webpage

Recommendations

Serbian Ministry of Health (Serbian MoH)

Location: Belgrade, Serbia

The Serbian MoH is the government body responsible for formulating and implementing national health policy, regulating the healthcare system, and ensuring the provision of healthcare services throughout Serbia. It oversees public health, healthcare institutions, pharmaceuticals, and national health programs.

Webpage

Recommendations

Schweizerische Gesellschaft für Psychiatrie und Psychotherapie (SGPP, Swiss Society for Psychiatry and Psychotherapy)

Location: Bern, Switzerland

The SGPP is the national society of psychiatrists practicing in Switzerland. It develops therapeutic and other recommenations for psychiatric and psychotherapeutic work, which helps to ensure the quality of care and to take into account new developments in an appropriate manner. Treatment recommendations are based on current scientific knowledge and procedures that have proven effective in practice. Where appropriate, they are based on internatuinally recognized guidelines.

Webpage

Recommendations

Singaporean Ministry of Health (Singaporean MoH)

Location: Singapore

The Ministry's mission is to promote good health and reduce illness, ensure access to good and affordable healthcare, and strive for medical excellence. For that, their Health Professionals Portal is a portal for the healthcare professionals to access multiple secure e-services and information, including guidelines.

Webpage

Recommendations

Socialstyrelsen (Socialstyrelsen)

Location: Stockholm, Sweden

The Socialstyrelsen is an agency that works to ensure good health social welfare and high-quality health and social care on equal terms for the whole Swedish population. They develop national guidelines as a support for those who make decisions concerning the allocation of resources within health and medical care and social services. The goal of these guidelines is to contribute towards patients and clients receiving a high standard of medical care and social services.

Webpage

Recommendations

Sundhedsstyrelsen (SST)

Location: Copenhagen, Denmark

The SST is a central authority in Denmark, responsible for advising, developing and administering the health sector. It regularly publishes reports, guidelines, recommendations and instructions. These publications cover topics such as prevention, treatment, organisation of the health service and public health. The purpose of the publications is to ensure an evidence-based and uniform approach to health and well-being in Denmark.

Webpage

Recommendations

Ugandan Ministry of Health (Ugandan MoH)

Location: Kampala, Uganda

The Ministry of Health is a government body mandated to provide stewardship and leadership of the health sector. It is responsible for policy review and development, supervision of health sector activities, engagement and dialogue with health development partners, strategic planning, standard setting and quality assurance, resource mobilization, advising other ministries, departments, and agencies on health-related matters, and ensuring quality, equity, and fairness in healthcare contributions. Its mission is to promote and ensure Universal Health Coverage through evidence-based and technically sound, client-centred policies, standards, and strategies. The Uganda Clinical Guidelines manual helps to achieve these standards by presenting updated, practical, and useful information on the diagnosis and management of common health conditions in Uganda. It also provides a rational basis for an efficient procurement and supply system that ensures the availability of safe, efficacious, quality medicines and health supplies.

Webpage

Recommendations

Department of Veterans Affairs (VADoD)

Location: Washington DC, United States

The Veterans Health Administration, in collaborations with the Department of Defense and other leading professional organizations, has been developing clinical practice guidelines since the early 1990s. Implementation of evidence-based clinical practice guidelines is one strategy VHA has embraced to improve care by reducing variation in practice and systematizing “best practices”. Guidelines address patient cohorts, serve to reduce errors, and provide consistent quality of care and utilization of resources throughout and between the VA and DOD health care systems. Guidelines are also cornerstones for accountability and facilitate learning and the conduct of research.

Webpage

Recommendations

World Federation of Biological Psychiatry (WFSBP)

Location: Munich, Germany

The WFSBP is a non-profit, international organization composed of 62 national societies of biological psychiatry, representing over 4,000 professionals. As a world authority on biological psychiatry, WFSBP is also highly commited to pioneering and promoting the highest levels of education and dissemination within the field. The development and formulation of the WFSBP Treatment Guidelines and Consensus Papers are a major focus of the federation. They are developed by the WFSBP Task Forces and provide a clear and top-quality treatment orientation for clinicians around the world.

Webpage

Recommendations

World Health Organization (WHO)

Location: Geneva, Switzerland

WHO leads global efforts to expand universal health coverage. They direct and coordinate the world’s response to health emergencies and promote healthier lives – from pregnancy care through old age. Their Triple Billion targets outline an ambitious plan for the world to achieve good health for all using science-based policies and programmes. For that, the Mental Health Gap Action Programme (mhGAP) guideline for mental, neurological and substance use disorders supports countries to strengthen capacity to deal with the growing burden of mental, neurological and substance use (MNS) conditions and narrow the treatment gap.

Webpage

Recommendations

Working group Development of Primary Care Guidelines (Worel)

Location: Antwerpen, Belgium

The Working Group Development of Primary Care Guidelines (WOREL) is a multidisciplinary scientific organization of guideline developers in Belgium, with experts from various primary care disciplines. The objective of WOREL is to develop and revise guidelines for healthcare professionals active in primary care; be the point of contact for projects in the field of developing guidelines for primary care; establish communication with the various guideline developing partners in Belgium; support the dissemination and incorporation of guidelines into various other EBP tools; use a common internationally recognized methodology for guideline development; involve patients and experts in the development of guidelines; follow international trends in the field of guideline development methodologies; set up and implement the 'living guidelines approach' for recommendations that are strongly subject to changing scientific insights; broaden the support base and network for guideline development and collaborate with other organizations in Europe, with a focus on exchanging knowledge and scientific substantiation.

Webpage

Recommendations
